description: Note from Barhun b. Yishaq al-Taharti, probably from al-Mahdiyya, to Nahray b. Nissim, Alexandria. Around 1045 (Gil). Nahray is in his early days in Egypt, and his cousin Barhun writes him instructions about buying flax, managing the goods that will arrive by ships, and dealing with the money that he will get from sales (information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 3, #378). Mentions harnawa, a name for aloeswood or ʿūd (see Blau s.v. harnawa) and דארכסה, which may be another aromatic wood (on the model of dār ṣīnī, literally Chinese bark, which is cinnamon). Information from Marina Rustow
